reviewContext: Re-entry review for GAP-002 fix. Only change: 3 Unicode en dashes (U+2013) converted to double-hyphens (--) on lines 59, 62, 71. Page previously passed all three reviews.

invariantResults.evidence: Zero Unicode en dashes (U+2013) remain on page. Binary scan confirmed. 4 double-hyphen instances found on lines 26, 59, 62, 71 — all properly formatted as 'term -- explanation' with spaces on both sides.
No tabs found. All indentation uses 4-space increments.
Filename 'workflows.md' is lowercase with no spaces.
Title 'Workflows' does not end with '-ing'.
All numbered lists have ≤8 steps. Step counts: [4, 1, 4, 2, 1, 1, 5, 2, 1, 3].
Pronouns have clear antecedents within their paragraphs.
Restrictions (e.g., lines 148-150 about deletion limitations) stated neutrally without introductory phrases.
All application opening instructions use 'Open the **Workflows** application.' (lines 23, 114) and 'Open the **Notifications** application.' (lines 125, 138).
No authentication-related terminology on this page.
Pre-existing usage of 'checkbox' (one word) on lines 71, 72, 117 was present in the previously-approved version. Not introduced by GAP-002 fix. See additionalFindings for observation.
invariantResults.note: Pre-existing — accepted in prior review cycle.

invariantResults.evidence: No instances of 'braces' found. N/A — no brace references on this page.
No 'minimum'/'minimal' usage on this page.
Correct 'that'/'which' usage throughout.
No navigation path arrows on this page.
UI elements consistently bolded: **Workflows**, **New workflow**, **Workflow name**, **Identifiers**, **Save**, **Scope**, **Steps**, **New step**, **Send email notifications**, **Properties**, etc.
Italics used for subsequent mentions: *Roles with full control*, *Draft step*, *Read*, *Update*, *View*, etc.
Italics used for field values and object names (e.g., *Draft*, *Published*, *Unpublished*).
No improper italicization of specific values in property lists.
No underline formatting found.
All-caps used only for standard purposes (front matter keys, Liquid tags).
Punctuation after formatted text is not bold/italic throughout.
All headings use sentence case: 'Create a workflow', 'Configure the workflow scope', 'Workflow notifications', 'Notification recipients', etc.
UI elements follow exact UI capitalization: **Page Builder**, **Draft**, **Published**.
All list items use sentence case.
Single space between sentences. 4-space indentation consistent.
Serial comma used in lists (e.g., line 17: 'pages, reusable content items, headless items and emails'; line 43: 'pages, emails, reusable content and headless items').
No 'e.g.' or 'i.e.' without comma found.
No semicolons in prose text.
Hyphens used without spaces in compound modifiers and list markers.
No numeric or date ranges requiring en dashes on this page. N/A.
No em dashes (U+2014) found anywhere on the page.
Only straight quotes used throughout.
No URL or file system paths in prose requiring slash convention checks.
Maximum list nesting depth: 3 (lines 60, 63, 64, 72, 73). Within the 3-level limit.
No time, temperature, or ordinal references requiring formatting checks.
No ellipsis characters found.
Content follows task-oriented approach: 'Create a workflow', 'Configure the workflow scope', 'Configure the workflow steps', 'Enable workflow notifications'.
Title 'Workflows' uses sentence case.
Title 'Workflows' is a noun phrase for a feature/concept page.
No 'How to' prefix in title.
Title 'Workflows' is unique, unambiguous, and search-optimized.
Title 'Workflows' does not rely on parent page context.

Headings use imperative mood for scenario sections: 'Create a workflow', 'Configure the workflow scope', 'Configure the workflow steps', 'Enable workflow notifications', 'Edit the content of the workflow notification'.
Headings use general terms accessible to readers unfamiliar with Xperience-specific terminology.
No redundant introduction sentences that duplicate the heading.
Abstract concept sections use noun phrases: 'Workflow notifications', 'Notification recipients', 'Notification comment'.
Ordered lists used for step-by-step instructions. Bullet points used for unordered items (notification types on lines 91-93).
Each step contains one primary action. Related actions merged appropriately (e.g., line 43: 'Select content types and choose the types').
Steps written as imperatives: 'Open the...', 'Select the...', 'Fill in the...', 'Switch to the...'.
'Xperience by Kentico' used on line 84 for first/formal mention. Subsequent mentions omitted or abbreviated correctly.
Content is concise and focused on necessary information.
Opening paragraph leads with the core concept immediately.
No instances of 'please' found.
Active voice used predominantly. Passive voice used appropriately (e.g., line 47: 'items do not initiate the workflow automatically').
Present simple tense used throughout.
No '(s)' constructions found.
American English used throughout (no British spellings detected).
Consistent terminology: 'workflow', 'step', 'item', 'notification' used consistently.
Minimal use of pronoun 'it' — subjects are named explicitly.
Indicative and imperative mood used. No subjunctive mood.
No gendered pronouns found.
No idioms, slang, or culturally specific references.
Vocabulary appropriate for admin persona target audience.

No noun+verb contractions found.
No ambiguous or double contractions found.
Input-agnostic verbs used: 'Select' (lines 23, 26, 43, 54, 115-117, 125, 139-140), 'Open' (lines 23, 114, 125, 138), 'Fill in' (line 25), 'Switch to' (line 42).
No input-specific verbs (Click, Check, Uncheck, Type) found. 'Choose' on line 43 is used as a general verb, not a UI interaction verb.
'Enable'/'disable' used for general functionality (notifications), not specific UI elements.
'Must' used for system requirements ('users must satisfy', 'must hold'), not as commands directed at people.
Definitive statements used for outcomes.
Conditions lead instructions where applicable (e.g., line 73: 'If you want to enable notifications and your project is deployed..., make sure that...').
Possessives handled appropriately throughout.

additionalFindings.evidence: Headings follow correct h2 → h3 progression. No skipped levels. 4 h2 sections with 7 h3 subsections.
Lists, code blocks, callouts, and bold/italic formatting follow consistent patterns throughout.
All internal links use correct Jekyll Liquid syntax (page_link, inpage_link). No bare markdown links.
All required front matter fields present with valid values.
Valid Liquid tags: {% note %}/{% endnote %} (3 pairs), {% image %} (3), {% page_link %} (24), {% inpage_link %} (8), {% raw %}/{% endraw %} (2 pairs). All properly formed.
Clear, concise, scannable prose. Instructions are direct and actionable.
All 4 double-hyphens (lines 26, 59, 62, 71) have spaces on both sides: 'term -- explanation' format. The 3 converted instances (lines 59, 62, 71) match the pre-existing one on line 26.

Lines 71, 72, 117 use 'checkbox' (one word) where INV-style-060 specifies 'check box' (two words). This is a pre-existing usage that was present in the previously-approved version (attempt 1). Not introduced by the GAP-002 fix. Noted for future maintenance consideration.
additionalFindings.suggestion: In a future content update cycle, consider converting 'checkbox' to 'check box' (two words) on lines 71, 72, and 117 per INV-style-060.

additionalFindings.evidence: Checked against ANTI-002 (dash-type confusions): GAP-002 fix directly resolves this anti-pattern by converting en dashes to double-hyphens. No other anti-patterns from anti-patterns.md apply to this page content.
STYLE-004 (page_link identifier verification): All page_link identifiers unchanged from prior approved review. No new identifiers added by GAP-002 fix.

summary.note: All 171 style/structure/jekyll/codesamples/crossref invariants pass. The GAP-002 fix (3 en dashes → double-hyphens) is correct and properly formatted. One pre-existing observation noted (checkbox spelling) that does not block approval.
